response: An important leader of the Democratic-Republican Party was Thomas Jefferson, who served as the third President of the United States and was a key founder of the party. Additionally, James Madison, who served as Secretary of State, was also a prominent leader of the party. Albert Gallatin, a significant figure in the party, held various federal positions and was a leading diplomat. Jeremiah Morrow, the last Democratic-Republican Governor of Ohio, was another important leader of the party.
